summary: This session discusses the differing motivations behind the Crusades among various classes of European society, including the nobility, Church, merchants, and peasantry. The user inquires about specific historical figures and the impact of the Crusades on trade and disease, highlighting the complex interplay of religious fervor, economic opportunity, and the risks involved.
facts: The user asked about the motivations behind the Crusades among different classes of European society. The user inquired about specific nobles or members of the Church motivated by the Crusades. The user questioned why the peasantry was motivated by religious fervor to join the Crusades despite the sacrifices involved. The user asked why merchants viewed the Crusades as an opportunity to expand trade networks despite the dangers of traveling through foreign lands. The user noted that the Crusades contributed to the spread of diseases like the bubonic plague due to large numbers of people traveling to and from the Holy Land.
